Title: The TuMYB46L-TuACO3 module regulates ethylene biosynthesis in einkorn wheat defense to powdery mildew

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of G1812 leaves with/without Bgt inoculation and with/without ethylene treatment; accession NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling leading to antifungal responses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in leaves after Bgt infection and after ethylene treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In T. urartu leaves, expression is reduced by silencing TuACO3 and elevated by silencing TuMYB46L
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; chitinase enzyme activity assay; expression analysis in TuACO3- and TuMYB46L-silenced plants and overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Act downstream of the TuMYB46L-TuACO3 module and are associated with enhanced antifungal defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Four chitinase genes are co-induced by Bgt infection and ethylene treatment. 2. Their expression decreases when TuACO3 is silenced and increases when TuMYB46L is silenced. 3. Total chitinase activity is promoted by Bgt, ethylene, and TuACO3 overexpression, but reduced by TuMYB46L overexpression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream defense markers for ethylene-mediated resistance modules in wheat
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes were identified as downstream effectors rather than primary causal genes of ethylene biosynthesis; specific individual gene functions were not separately dissected in this study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid (ACC) to ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as the last step of ethylene biosynthesis; critical for infection-induced ethylene increase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes wheat defense against powdery mildew caused by Blumeria graminis f.sp. tritici
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in leaves after Bgt infection; elevated at 24 hpi in six T. urartu accessions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High/induced in infected leaves; overexpression in transgenic common wheat increases basal ethylene production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blumeria graminis f.sp. tritici infection; exogenous ethylene; ACC; AVG
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; immunoblotting; transient overexpression; TIGS; BSMV-VIGS; stable overexpression in transgenic wheat; gas chromatography ethylene measurement; pathogen phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; wheat ubiquitin promoter in transient assay (pUbi)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of G1812 leaves with/without Bgt inoculation and with/without ethylene treatment; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TuACO3 is induced by Bgt infection in Triticum urartu leaves and its protein accumulation rises at 24 hpi. 2. Silencing TuACO3 lowers ethylene production and increases susceptibility to Bgt. 3. Overexpression of TuACO3 in common wheat elevates ethylene and enhances resistance to Bgt.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene accumulation and enhances defense against Bgt
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or developmental defects observed in transgenic overexpression lines under glasshouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering elevated disease-associated ethylene production and powdery mildew resistance in wheat; suitable for transgenic stacking or activation editing with caution about ethylene-related pleiotropy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog in common wheat is TaACO3 (TraesCS6A02G325600); expression is negatively regulated by TuMYB46L; silencing represses downstream chitinase genes and total chitinase activity

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Blumeria graminis f.sp. tritici infection rapidly downregulates TuMYB46L expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression through direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TuACO3 transcription by binding MYB46 cis-elements in the TuACO3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator whose downregulation is required for infection-induced ethylene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes wheat defense against powdery mildew
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly and rapidly decreased in leaves after Bgt infection across six T. urartu accessions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced by pathogen infection in einkorn wheat; transgenic overexpression in common wheat suppresses infection-induced ethylene increase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; promoter-reporter assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; TIGS; BSMV-VIGS; stable overexpression in common wheat; qRT-PCR; ethylene measurement; pathogen phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of G1812 leaves with/without Bgt inoculation and with/without ethylene treatment; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TuMYB46L directly binds multiple MYB46 recognition sites in the TuACO3 promoter. 2. Bgt infection rapidly decreases TuMYB46L expression, relieving repression of TuACO3. 3. Silencing TuMYB46L increases TuACO3 expression, ethylene content, chitinase gene expression and resistance to Bgt, whereas overexpression causes the opposite effects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates ethylene accumulation and powdery mildew defense by repressing TuACO3
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or developmental defects reported for common wheat overexpression lines under glasshouse conditions; authors discuss possible growth-defense tradeoffs as future consideration
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for knockout or promoter editing to derepress TuACO3 and enhance ethylene-mediated powdery mildew resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically related to Arabidopsis AtMYB46 but functionally characterized here in ethylene-mediated defense; ortholog in common wheat is TaMYB46L (TraesCS5A02G101000); altered expression also changes downstream chitinase genes and total chitinase activity
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for stable overexpression; 35S promoter for reporter assay effector; wheat ubiquitin promoter (pUbi) for transient assays
